product_overview: A web-based Pokémon team builder that supports Generations 1–5 with drag-and-drop team construction, type coverage analysis, AI coaching, battle planning, and account-backed team persistence. The app is built with Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, and Tailwind and delivers static generation for game pages plus client-side interactive tooling.
core_goals: Enable fast, flexible construction of 6-Pokémon teams across mainline games (Gens 1–5).
Provide clear defensive and offensive type coverage analysis and actionable recommendations to patch weaknesses.
Support persistence and sharing of teams via authenticated Save/Load and a My Teams dashboard.
Offer advanced tooling (Battle Planner, AI Coach) to analyze matchups and provide strategy guidance.
Keep client interactions responsive (drag-and-drop, search, command palette) and protect key flows with authentication where required.

features.name: Game Selection
features.description: Pick one of the supported mainline Pokémon games (Gens 1–5) to start building a team for that generation.
user_flows: Navigate to /play -> View grid of available Pokemon games -> Click on a game card (e.g., Gen 1) -> Navigate to /game/gen1 -> See the Team Builder for that generation
Navigate to /play -> View grid of available Pokemon games -> Click on a game card -> Generation page fails to load -> See an error banner 'Unable to load team builder' or retry CTA

features.name: Pokemon Team Builder
features.description: Interactive 6-slot team builder with search, regional/national Pokedex toggle, version exclusivity filters, drag-and-drop, and undo/redo.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Focus search bar -> Type 'Pikachu' -> See filtered results update -> Click a Pokemon card -> Pokemon is added to the selected empty team slot
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Toggle dex mode from regional to national -> Change version filter to 'Red' -> See selection list update to version-appropriate Pokemon
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Drag 'Bulbasaur' from selection list -> Drop onto team slot 2 -> See 'Bulbasaur' occupy slot 2 -> Drag slot 2 to slot 1 -> See Pokemon reorder
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Search for a non-existent string 'xyz' -> See message 'No Pokemon found' -> Attempt to drag -> No Pokemon is added

features.name: Defensive Type Coverage
features.description: Heatmap visualization of team weaknesses and resistances across all 18 types, shown when the team has at least one member.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Add at least one Pokemon to the team -> Defensive coverage component loads dynamically -> See heatmap with per-type counts
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Add at least one Pokemon -> Hover over 'Fire' cell in the heatmap -> See tooltip showing weakness/resistance counts
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> With an empty team -> Defensive coverage does not render -> See prompt 'Add a Pokemon to view defensive coverage'

features.name: Offensive Type Coverage
features.description: Display of which types the team can hit super-effectively (STAB and non-STAB) to evaluate offensive coverage.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Add multiple Pokemon to the team -> Offensive coverage panel calculates coverage -> See list or visualization of types the team can hit super-effectively
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Team is empty -> Open Offensive coverage area -> See message 'Add Pokemon to show offensive coverage'

features.name: Team Recommendations (Smart Picks)
features.description: Recommend Pokemon that best patch the team's defensive weaknesses using type analysis and score-ranked suggestions.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Add current team members -> Open Team Recommendations -> See ranked list of recommended Pokemon with scores
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Team Recommendations -> Click a recommended Pokemon -> Recommended Pokemon is added to the first available slot
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Team already has 6 Pokemon -> Open Team Recommendations -> Click a recommended Pokemon -> See prompt 'Replace an existing slot or cancel' or an inline 'Team full' notice

features.name: Battle Planner
features.description: Analyze team matchups against gym leaders, Elite Four, and Champions with matchup matrices, slot assignment recommendations, and saveable opponent presets.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Battle Planner tab in the builder -> Click Boss Preset Picker -> Select a gym preset -> See populated opponent team and matchup matrix
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Battle Planner tab -> Toggle realism from Sandbox to Strict -> See matchup predictions update according to game rules
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Battle Planner tab -> Click Analyze -> POST to /api/battle/matchups/analyze -> See win/loss predictions and recommended assignments
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Battle Planner tab -> Click Analyze without selecting opponent or preset -> See validation error 'Select an opponent team or preset before analysis'
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Battle Planner tab -> Click Analyze -> API returns error -> See error banner 'Unable to analyze matchups, try again'

features.name: Capture Guide
features.description: Show where to find or capture each team Pokémon for the selected game version via the Capture Guide panel.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Capture Guide panel -> Select a game version from the version filter -> See capture locations listed for each Pokemon on the team
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open Capture Guide panel -> Select a version that lacks location data for a Pokemon -> See placeholder 'Capture location unknown' for that Pokemon

features.name: AI Coach Panel
features.description: Contextual AI chat assistant that gives team-specific advice, answers strategy questions, and performs explicit team analyses with usage quota tracking.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open the AI Coach panel -> Type a question about 'lead selection' -> Click Send -> POST to /api/ai/chat -> See AI response appear in the conversation
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open the AI Coach panel -> Click 'Analyze team' -> POST to /api/ai/analyze -> See structured analysis generated for the current team and usage quota update

Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Open the AI Coach panel -> Send message but usage quota is exhausted -> Receive quota error response from /api/ai/chat -> See message 'Quota exceeded' and guidance to wait or upgrade

features.name: Save and Load Teams
features.description: Persist teams to the backend for reuse across sessions; saved teams are accessible in-builder and via the My Teams dashboard. Requires authentication.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Click Save Team button -> App detects user is not signed in -> Redirect to /auth or show sign-in modal -> Sign in -> Return to /game/gen1 -> Click Save Team again -> Enter team name -> Confirm -> POST to /api/teams -> See saved team in Saved Teams panel
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Click Saved Teams panel -> Click a saved team -> GET /api/teams -> Load saved team into builder -> See team populate slots
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Click Save Team -> Sign in flow is cancelled -> See message 'You must be signed in to save teams' and save is aborted
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Click Save Team -> Submit team name -> API returns error -> See error banner 'Unable to save team, try again'

features.name: My Teams Dashboard
features.description: Authenticated dashboard showing all saved teams grouped by generation with quick actions to open in the builder or delete.
user_flows: Navigate to /teams -> If authenticated, GET /api/teams -> See teams grouped by generation with team cards showing sprites and type badges
Navigate to /teams -> Click 'Open in Builder' on a team card -> Navigate to /game/gen{n} -> Load the selected team into the builder
Navigate to /teams -> Click delete on a team card -> Click confirm on second confirmation -> DELETE /api/teams/{id} -> See team removed from the list
Navigate to /teams while not authenticated -> App redirects to /auth -> See sign-in page

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Sign in or sign up using email/username and password with real-time username availability checks during sign-up.
user_flows: Navigate to /auth -> Select Sign Up tab -> Fill username field -> See real-time availability check via GET /api/auth/check-username -> Fill email and password fields -> Click Submit -> POST to /api/auth/sign-up/email -> See success and redirect to user area
Navigate to /auth -> Select Sign In tab -> Fill email/username and password -> Click Submit -> POST to /api/auth/sign-in/email -> Successful sign-in redirects to /teams or prior location
Navigate to /auth -> Sign In with invalid credentials -> POST to /api/auth/sign-in/email -> Receive error -> See inline 'Invalid credentials' message

features.name: Public Trainer Profile
features.description: Public-facing trainer pages that show avatar, bio, favorite games/Pokemon, and public saved teams.
user_flows: Navigate to /u/ashketchum -> GET /api/profile/{username} -> See trainer avatar, bio, favorite games and Pokemon, and list of public saved teams
Navigate to /u/nonexistent -> GET /api/profile/{username} -> Receive 404 -> See 'Profile not found' page

features.name: Profile Settings
features.description: Authenticated settings area for editing username, bio, avatar, favorites, and featured team with live validation and character counters.
user_flows: Navigate to /settings/profile -> GET /api/profile/me -> Edit username field -> See real-time availability check via GET /api/auth/check-username -> Update bio with character counter visible -> Select avatar and favorites -> Click Save -> PUT to /api/profile/me -> See confirmation 'Profile updated'
Navigate to /settings/profile -> Change username to one that is taken -> Real-time check indicates 'Username unavailable' -> Prevent save and show inline error

features.name: Onboarding Tour
features.description: Interactive guided tour overlay on first visit to the builder to highlight core features and controls.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 on first visit -> Onboarding tooltip overlay appears -> Click Next through tour steps -> Click Finish -> Tour dismisses and preference saved
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Onboarding tooltip appears -> Click Skip -> Tour dismisses immediately and will not auto-show again

features.name: Command Palette
features.description: Keyboard-driven quick launcher (Cmd+K) to search commands, Pokemon, and navigate quickly.
user_flows: Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Press Cmd+K -> Command Palette opens -> Type 'Pikachu' -> Press Enter -> Execute selected command or focus Pokemon in selection list
Navigate to /game/gen1 -> Press Cmd+K -> Command Palette opens -> Type nonsense 'asdfqwer' -> See 'No commands found' -> Press Escape to close palette

features.name: Admin Dashboard (Settings Admin)
features.description: Restricted admin settings page for owners/administrators to perform site-level actions (may be partially UI-only).
user_flows: Navigate to /settings/admin while authenticated as ADMIN/OWNER -> GET relevant admin data -> See admin controls and perform CRUD actions in the UI
Navigate to /settings/admin while authenticated as non-admin -> See 'Access denied' or restricted view

Navigate to /settings/admin -> Perform an admin CRUD action in the UI -> UI updates locally but backend endpoint is missing or not wired -> See visual change but receive warning 'Some admin actions may be local-only'

routes.description: Landing page with hero section, features showcase, and CTA buttons
Game selector - pick from 5 mainline Pokemon games (Gens 1-5)
Main team builder - RSC page that statically generates team builder for each generation
Authentication page with Sign In and Sign Up tabs
My Teams dashboard - view and manage all saved teams grouped by generation
Public trainer profile page with bio, favorite games/Pokemon, and saved teams
Settings hub with Privacy Controls, Account settings, and Legal links
Profile customization - edit username, bio, avatar, favorite games/Pokemon, featured team
Admin dashboard restricted to ADMIN/OWNER roles
Privacy policy page with cookie preferences

features.name: Game Selection
features.description: User selects a mainline Pokemon game (Gens 1-5) to start building a team
user_interactions: View grid of available Pokemon games
Click on a game card to navigate to the team builder for that game

features.name: Pokemon Team Builder
features.description: Build a 6-Pokemon team using drag-and-drop, search, and filter by version/dex
user_interactions: Search for Pokemon by name in the search bar
Toggle between regional and national Pokedex mode
Filter Pokemon by game version exclusives
Click or drag a Pokemon to add it to a team slot
Remove a Pokemon from a team slot by clicking the remove button
Drag Pokemon between team slots to reorder
Undo/redo team changes using keyboard shortcut or button

features.name: Defensive Type Coverage
features.description: Heatmap visualization showing team weaknesses and resistances across all 18 types
user_interactions: View defensive coverage heatmap when team has at least one Pokemon
Hover over type cells to see weakness/resistance counts

features.name: Offensive Type Coverage
features.description: Shows which types the team can hit super-effectively (STAB coverage)
user_interactions: View offensive coverage after adding Pokemon to team
See which types the team covers offensively

features.name: Team Recommendations (Smart Picks)
features.description: Recommends Pokemon that best patch team weaknesses based on type analysis
user_interactions: View recommended Pokemon filtered by team composition
Click a recommended Pokemon to add it to the team

features.name: Battle Planner
features.description: Analyze team matchups against gym leaders, Elite 4, and Champion with win predictions
user_interactions: Open Battle Planner tab
Select a boss preset (gym/elite4/champion) or create manual opponent team
View matchup matrix showing win/loss predictions for each pairing
View recommended slot assignments for maximum win probability
Toggle between Strict (game rules) and Sandbox (analysis) realism modes
Save custom opponent teams for reuse

features.name: Capture Guide
features.description: Shows where to find/capture each team Pokemon in the selected game version
user_interactions: Select a game version from the version filter
View capture locations for each Pokemon in the team

features.name: AI Coach Panel
features.description: AI chat assistant with contextual team analysis and coaching advice
user_interactions: Open the AI Coach panel
Send a message about the team or strategy
Receive AI response with team-specific advice
Request team analysis
View usage quota indicator

features.name: Save and Load Teams
features.description: Persist teams to backend and reload them across sessions
user_interactions: Click Save Team button
Enter team name
View list of saved teams
Load a saved team by clicking it
Delete a saved team with confirmation

features.name: My Teams Dashboard
features.description: View and manage all saved teams grouped by game generation
user_interactions: View all saved teams grouped by generation
See team card with Pokemon sprites and type badges
Click Open in Builder to load team in the team builder
Delete a team with two-click confirmation safety

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Sign in or sign up with email/username and password
user_interactions: Select Sign In or Sign Up tab
Fill in email or username field
Fill in password field
For Sign Up, fill in username and confirm password
Click submit button
See error message on invalid credentials
See real-time username availability indicator during sign up

features.name: Public Trainer Profile
features.description: View a user's public profile with bio, favorites, and shared teams
user_interactions: View trainer avatar with frame styling
See bio and member since date
View favorite games and Pokemon
Browse all public saved teams
View featured team

features.name: Profile Settings
features.description: Customize user profile including avatar, username, bio, and favorites
user_interactions: Change username with real-time availability check
Edit bio with character counter
Select avatar from picker with frame options
Choose up to 5 favorite games
Select up to 8 favorite Pokemon
Set a featured team
Copy public profile URL
Save all changes

features.name: Onboarding Tour
features.description: Interactive walkthrough for new users to learn key features
user_interactions: View onboarding tooltip overlay on first visit
Click Next to advance through tour steps
Click Skip to dismiss the tour

features.name: Command Palette
features.description: Keyboard-driven quick navigation and action launcher (Cmd+K)
user_interactions: Press Cmd+K to open command palette
Type to search commands or Pokemon
Press Enter or click to execute command
Press Escape to close

known_limitations.issue: Admin CRUD operations may be UI-only and not fully connected to backend
known_limitations.impact: Some admin actions might only update local state

known_limitations.issue: AI Coach requires usage quota; heavy users may hit rate limits
known_limitations.impact: AI coaching features unavailable after quota exceeded

known_limitations.issue: Battle Planner presets only available for Gen 3 Hoenn games currently
known_limitations.impact: Battle preset picker limited to specific games; manual input required for others
